danger_signals.visual_form: Cockpit HUD display with targeted enemy vessel visible in viewport
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; enemy has weapon lock or is closing distance

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinctive feline-design enemy fighter silhouettes; aggressive attack patterns in space
danger_signals.meaning: Predatory alien faction actively engaged in warfare; extreme danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Failed objectives reflected in mission debrief; assignment to inferior ships next mission
danger_signals.meaning: Strategic setback; world-state shifting toward losing campaign path

danger_signals.visual_form: AI-controlled wingman absence from subsequent missions; permanent roster loss
danger_signals.meaning: Irreversible loss of combat support; escalating danger for player

safety_signals.visual_form: Medal awards and rank promotions in debrief screens
safety_signals.meaning: Player advancement; access to superior ships and equipment next mission

safety_signals.visual_form: Bengal-class Strike Carrier (TCS Tiger's Claw) as player base; allied pilots and vessels
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; organized military structure; faction support

safety_signals.visual_form: Upgraded fighter craft cockpit; improved weapon and armor visuals
safety_signals.meaning: Performance-based reward; player is on winning campaign path

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bengal-class Strike Carrier (TCS Tiger's Claw); multinational pilot roster
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military carrier bridge; standardized fighter designs; organized mission structure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Feline warrior race aesthetic; predatory ship designs; god-of-war iconography (Sivar dreadnought, Vigrid system operations)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard fighters (Dralthi, Dralthi II), Heavy fighters (Hhriss-class), Heavy carriers (Snakeir-class), Dreadnoughts (Sivar-class with graviton weapon)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Aggressive swarm tactics; weapon escalation visible in successive encounters; presence indicates active war zone

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bird-like intelligent lifeform; negotiating colonial allegiance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Firekka System; civilian population threatened by Kilrathi presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player receives medals, promotions, superior ships; mission assignments become strategically offensive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Campaign culminates in strike against Kilrathi High Command starbase in Venice system; Kilrathi forced to retreat; canon ending

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player assigned inferior ships; mission assignments become increasingly defensive
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Human refugees abandon sector; TCS Tiger's Claw forced to retreat; strategic territory loss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goddard colony destroyed in Secret Missions; quarter-million civilian casualties; wreckage and corpses visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Kilrathi weapon escalation (Graviton weapon deployment); new ship classes appear; strategic threat assessment increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Firekkans negotiating confederation membership; unusual Kilrathi military buildup in Firekka System
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Massive Kilrathi battle group forms; upgraded and novel ship classes deployed; Terrans forced to retreat despite Firekkan alliance signing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kilrathi lord Ralgha nar Hhallas breaks from enemy faction; brings Fralth-class vessel
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Political schism in Kilrathi hierarchy; potential narrative pivot point

environmental_storytelling.example: Goddard colony destruction narrative reveals quarter-million dead; establishes Kilrathi capacity for genocide and weapon sophistication
Progression from standard Kilrathi fighters to Dralthi variants, then Sivar-class dreadnoughts with graviton weapons; visual demonstration of enemy technological advancement
Successful missions lead to offensive strikes on enemy high command; failed missions lead to defensive retreats and civilian evacuation; world-state visual shifts based on player effectiveness
Killed AI wingmen are gone forever from roster; empty cockpit wingman slot signals accumulated losses over campaign
Campaign moves across multiple planets and systems (Venice, Goddard, Vigrid, Firekka); expanding Kilrathi presence visually demonstrates spreading conflict
TCS Tiger's Claw serves as constant player home base; its retreat or continued operation reflects campaign trajectory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Aggressive pursuit; coordinated attack patterns; multiple interceptor waves indicate escalating threat level
Concentrated presence in key systems (Firekka, Venice); escalating dreadnought and heavy carrier deployments indicate major offensive preparation
Promotion through ranks; assignment to strike missions against enemy leadership; increasingly strategic mission objectives
Demotion to defensive missions; inferior ship allocation; retreat orders; evacuation escort missions
Mass evacuation and abandonment of sector during losing campaign path; Goddard colony destroyed in expansion narrative
Initially neutral; sign confederation articles; subsequently become refugees requiring Terran protection as Kilrathi battle group arrives

notes: Wing Commander uses a dynamic world-state system where visual manifestations (ship assignments, mission locations, debrief medals) directly communicate campaign trajectory. The branching narrative allows players to switch between winning and losing paths, with each path having distinct visual/behavioral signatures. Faction identity is primarily conveyed through enemy ship design aesthetics (feline Kilrathi vs. geometric Terran designs) and narrative scale (genocide, weapon escalation, territorial expansion). The permanent wingman death mechanic creates visible roster attrition that compounds danger signals over time. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on mission debrief narratives and geographic expansion rather than explorable environments, fitting the simulation paradigm.
